avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The widespread unstable windslab present mainly above 900m on mostly South-Westerly to Northerly aspects will become increasingly unstable as it thaws. Gullies, Coire headwalls, crag aprons especially affected. Large cornices overhanging these aspects will be prone to collapse triggering slopes below. At lower elevations and above 500m, isolated windslab deposits in steep wind sheltered places such as steep banks and burn lines will be unstable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Freezing level will be around 1200m throughout the period. Precipitation will be persistent sleet and snow at summit levels rain below. Winds remaining South-Easterly strong to gale force on the tops.
Observed Weather Influences: Winds strong South-Easterly at first, gradually lessened in the day. Drifting at higher elevations. Freezing level gradually rising to 900m or so as the day progressed. Light precipitation throughout the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Deep and widespread unstable windslab is present mostly above 900 metres on Westerly to Northerly aspects, with cross loaded deposits affecting South-West and North-East aspects. Gullies, Coire headwalls, crag aprons especially affected.  Large fragile cornices overhang these slopes. Above 500m localised moderately bonded windslab deposits are present in isolated steep wind sheltered places such as steep banks and burn lines. Elsewhere wind scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of well bonded windslab will remain in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres. As the day progresses the snowpack will consolidate, becoming firm and icy above 1100 metres. Above 650 metres and below 1100 metres snow loss is anticipated, resulting in moist snow with residual snow patches in hollows and burn lines. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Precipitation is expected through most of the forecast period. This will be a mix of summit snow and rain at all elevations as the freezing level fluctuates around the highest summits. Winds will be Easterly strong, backing to the South-South-East and becoming fresh. During the day the freezing level will be around 1200 to 1150 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: A generally dry day with strong Easterly winds. Although a dusting of new snow fell above 550 metres on the southern boundary of the area. The freezing level was around 800 metres in the morning rising to 1000 metres later.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of windslab are present on wind sheltered locations of a West to North aspects above 900 metres. These are mostly well bonded, with some isolated and avoidable accumulations demonstrating weakness in the south of the Creag Meagaidh area. Outwith these areas slopes exposed to the wind are icy and scoured. Elsewhere a dusting of new snow is rapidly thawing below in 900 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Wet snow instabilities will become increasingly prevalent and avalanches are likely, particularly on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. The greatest instability will be located in areas such as corrie head walls, gullies and convexities. Cornices will be prone to collapse. Between 500m and 900m isolated wet snow instabilities will be present, mainly on steep banks and burn lines. Wind exposed slopes will be generally stable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A band of showery rain arrives during the evening and persists throughout the period, falling as sleet initially and then rain to the higher tops overnight and into the morning. Strong East-South-Easterly winds will veer more Southerly through the day. The freezing level will be above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: Fresh snowfall overnight, lying to around 400 metres. Mostly dry through the day with fresh Easterly winds. The freezing level was initially at 500 metres and gradually rose to around 900 metres by the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: There has been some consolidation of recent deposits of unstable windslab. However, weaknesses continue to be present, particularly on steep South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Cornices are weak and prone to collapse. Below this altitude on steep convex terrain and burn lines moderately bonded windslab continues to consolidate. Wind exposed terrain is firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The generally firm snowpack will slowly soften in the milder conditions. Stability will generally be good throughout. Any fresh deposits of wet snow on the highest tops will be shallow and not significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be milder than of late with the freezing level around 1200 metres. Easterly winds will veer to the South through the day. There may be some light precipitation which will fall as damp snow at the highest levels.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cloudy but generally dry day. Strong winds were South-East. The freezing level crept up to about 1000 metres through the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Patches of windslab remain on some sheltered West to North aspects above 800 metres. However, these areas are not extensive, and are slowly stabilising the the relatively mild conditions. On other aspects, and at lower levels, the snowpack is firm and stable.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded, firm and stable in all locations. Any new wet snow deposits on the highest summits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Drizzle at all levels may turn to wet snow on the highest summits towards the end of the day. The freezing level will be above all summits falling to around 1050 metres.  Fresh Easterly winds becoming South-South-Easterly during the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A mostly dry day with only occasional light snow flurries above 500 metres in the morning. Very strong mainly Easterly winds. The freezing level rose to near the highest summits in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. A good cover of very firm snow remains on most aspects above 700 metres  with patchier cover below.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Milder conditions with light rain at lower levels will moisten much of the snow. Higher slopes will remain firm possibly icy in places.  Localised areas of windslab will exist in sheltered gullies and on slopes mainly above 1000 metres with a South-West through North-West to Northerly aspect. Gully exits and steep corrie rims will be most affected. Some fragile cornices may also exist in these areas.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Light falls of new snow will affect the higher slopes overnight and through Sunday. Winds will remain generally Easterly but veering Southerly later in the afternoon. Winds will be fresh to strong overnight but reducing through the day. The freezing level will be around 1300 metres overnight lowering to around 1100 metres later in the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Light new snow overnight affected slopes mainly above 700 metres. Easterly winds were strong overnight becoming moderate to fresh today.  Summit temperatures today are around -2 degrees C.
ObservedSnowStability: Much of the snowpack base is re-frozen and firm with widespread rime and good general stability. Areas of moderately bonded windslab exist in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a South-West to North-Westerly aspect, these areas tend to be localised mainly to gully exits and steep corrie rims. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
